memory.summary: I have a free afternoon before the term project is due Thursday, and I can choose between leisure or quickly finishing an easy chore.
The easy chore is now 4% complete, confirming it can be finished quickly without draining my low energy.
The easy chore is progressing slowly, taking 0.5 hours for only a 5% gain, which may not be the most efficient use of my remaining energy.
The easy chore is progressing too slowly, yielding only a 4% gain in half an hour while the afternoon slips away and the main project remains untouched.
I made progress on the Term project, but I realize that neglecting the future project now will compound stress and require significantly more time later.
The trivial chore consumed 0.5 hours for a mere 3% gain, confirming my suspicion that this approach is inefficient given my low energy and existing stress.
The trivial chore consumed another 0.5 hours for only a 2% gain, confirming that this low-yield task is draining my limited energy without meaningfully advancing my goals.
This trivial chore yielded only 3% progress in 0.5 hours, confirming that low-yield tasks are inefficiently draining my already depleted energy and increasing stress.
The trivial chore consumed another 0.5 hours for only a 2% gain, confirming that this low-yield task is draining my limited energy without meaningfully advancing my goals.
After three sessions, this trivial chore has yielded only 2% progress while draining my energy and raising stress, confirming it is an inefficient use of my time.
After four sessions, this trivial chore has yielded only 2% progress while draining my energy and raising stress, confirming it is an inefficient use of my time.
